g.title: Zhara'kul
The Sable Wastes
The Furnace of Heaven
Ash-Zurab Built into a volcanic rift; here stands the Temple of Red Prophecy, where the High Oracle drinks molten brass to gain visions.

Asmarat Asmarat sits in a great oxbow of the River Aset. Its skyline bristles with brass obelisks and mirror-domed temples, all aligned to celestial nodes once calculated by dream-astronomers of the old age.

Kherum-Zan Kherum-Zan rests on the central floodplain of the River Mereth, built atop a great natural rise shaped into seven terraced platforms, echoing the Descent Cycle of the Sky-Born. It is the ritual, scholarly, and mnemonic heart of the Myrian Halls, considered sacred by all city-states in the coalition.
Zin-Ravan The radiant capital; home to the Vault of Echoes and seat of the Shahenshar
Tirazûn A seaside village of abundant trade but scarce beauty.
The Black Groves of Haran’Set
The Brazen Steps A riverside town of scarce magic. Touchstone: a famous tavern.
Sul-Hajra Artisan satrapy famed for its True Brass forges and hereditary mirror-casters
Throsim’s Gate A mountain-ringed monastery-town, cloaked in mist and riddled with prayer-chambers cut into the stone.
Ur-Kelai A seaside city of abundant knowledge and scarce privacy.
Namarra A quiet pilgrimage village overlooking an inland salt sea, said to echo with whispers from beyond time.
Hold of Three Seals A busy port city of abundant leadership and scarce freedom.
Athribdos Port-town and trade hub for Ta'ren
Dur-Zama A busy seaside town of abundant happyness. Touchstone: a curiosity shop.
Qazhadûn Pilgrimage city built around a vein of exposed True Brass; shrine-keepers demand steep tribute.
Omesh-in-Flame A once-rebellious city now perpetually alight with votive fire and occupied by imperial priests.
Zirana’s Hook Cliffside port notorious for free captains, coral coinage, and storm-born pacts made in salt temples.
Tirazun (South)
Lomarun Town of bells and sails, its skyline chimes with the movement of ships, tides, and secrets.
Fallen Zahr al-Narās A city of abundant food and scarce safety. Touchstone: a festival.
Selareth A desert outpost, built around a small oasis
Zanarth A small riverside village
Kalzur-Hem The last civilized place before the desert
